model_narrative: We operate a small power grid over a single day broken into 24 hourly periods. In each hour we decide how much power to generate from wind, solar, and a fuel cell, how much power to send into or draw out of a battery, and whether the battery is set to charge or to discharge that hour. We also account for any demand we fail to serve and any surplus generation that has to be dumped. Generation, charging, discharging, unserved demand, and surplus each carry their own per-unit cost that can vary by hour. The objective is to minimize the total operating cost over the day.

sets.name: T
sets.doc: the hours of the day in chronological order, indexed by the integers 1 through 24; the first member is the opening hour and each later member follows the one before it

params.name: power_lim_wind
params.doc: the maximum wind power that can be generated in each hour, in power units

params.name: power_lim_pv
params.doc: the maximum solar (photovoltaic) power that can be generated in each hour, in power units

params.name: power_lim_fuel
params.doc: the maximum fuel-cell power that can be generated in each hour, in power units; equal to 80 for every hour

params.name: power_lim_charge
params.doc: the maximum power that can flow into the battery while charging in each hour, in power units; equal to 200 for every hour

params.name: power_lim_discharge
params.doc: the maximum power that can flow out of the battery while discharging in each hour, in power units; equal to 50 for every hour

params.name: power_lim_battery
params.doc: the maximum amount of energy the battery can hold in each hour, in power units; equal to 200 for every hour

params.name: initial_battery
params.doc: the amount of energy stored in the battery at the very start, before the first hour, in power units; a scalar equal to 100

params.name: load_demand
params.doc: the electricity load that must be served in each hour, in power units

params.name: cost_wind
params.doc: the unit cost of wind generation in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_pv
params.doc: the unit cost of solar generation in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_fuel
params.doc: the unit cost of fuel-cell generation in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_charge
params.doc: the unit cost of charging the battery in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_discharge
params.doc: the unit cost of discharging the battery in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_undelivered
params.doc: the penalty cost for each unit of demand left unserved in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_excess
params.doc: the unit cost charged for surplus generation that has to be dumped in each hour, in cost per power unit; equal to 0 for every hour

vars.name: wind
vars.doc: the wind power actually generated in each hour, in power units

vars.name: pv
vars.doc: the solar power actually generated in each hour, in power units

vars.name: fuel
vars.doc: the fuel-cell power actually generated in each hour, in power units

vars.name: charge
vars.doc: the power sent into the battery to charge it in each hour, in power units

vars.name: discharge
vars.doc: the power drawn out of the battery in each hour, in power units

vars.name: undelivered
vars.doc: the amount of demand left unserved in each hour, in power units

vars.name: excess
vars.doc: the surplus generation that exceeds what is needed and must be dumped in each hour, in power units

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the discharge indicator for each hour; equals 1 if the battery is allowed to discharge that hour and 0 otherwise

vars.name: y
vars.doc: the charge indicator for each hour; equals 1 if the battery is allowed to charge that hour and 0 otherwise

vars.name: battery
vars.doc: the energy stored in the battery at the end of each hour, in power units

vars.name: tc
vars.doc: the total operating cost over the whole day, in cost units

description: The battery can only draw power out when it is set to discharge that hour, and even then the power drawn out cannot exceed the discharging limit. In every hour the power discharged is held to zero unless the battery is in discharge mode, and when it is in discharge mode it is capped at the discharging limit for that hour.

description: The battery can only take power in when it is set to charge that hour, and even then the power taken in cannot exceed the charging limit. params.name: cost_fuel
params.doc: the unit cost of fuel-cell generation in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_charge
params.doc: the unit cost of charging the battery in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_discharge
params.doc: the unit cost of discharging the battery in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_undelivered
params.doc: the penalty cost for each unit of demand left unserved in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_excess
params.doc: the unit cost charged for surplus generation that has to be dumped in each hour, in cost per power unit; equal to 0 for every hour

vars.name: wind
vars.doc: the wind power actually generated in each hour, in power units

vars.name: pv
vars.doc: the solar power actually generated in each hour, in power units

vars.name: fuel
vars.doc: the fuel-cell power actually generated in each hour, in power units

vars.name: charge
vars.doc: the power sent into the battery to charge it in each hour, in power units

vars.name: discharge
vars.doc: the power drawn out of the battery in each hour, in power units

vars.name: undelivered
vars.doc: the amount of demand left unserved in each hour, in power units

vars.name: excess
vars.doc: the surplus generation that exceeds what is needed and must be dumped in each hour, in power units

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the discharge indicator for each hour; equals 1 if the battery is allowed to discharge that hour and 0 otherwise

vars.name: y
vars.doc: the charge indicator for each hour; equals 1 if the battery is allowed to charge that hour and 0 otherwise

vars.name: battery
vars.doc: the energy stored in the battery at the end of each hour, in power units

vars.name: tc
vars.doc: the total operating cost over the whole day, in cost units

description: In every hour the power sent into the battery added to the energy already stored coming into that hour cannot exceed the battery's storage capacity for that hour. The energy already stored coming into the hour is the level carried over from the end of the previous hour, except in the very first hour where it is the starting battery level.

model_narrative: We operate a small power grid over a single day broken into 24 hourly periods. In each hour we decide how much power to generate from wind, solar, and a fuel cell, how much power to send into or draw out of a battery, and whether the battery is set to charge or to discharge that hour. We also account for any demand we fail to serve and any surplus generation that has to be dumped. Generation, charging, discharging, unserved demand, and surplus each carry their own per-unit cost that can vary by hour. The objective is to minimize the total operating cost over the day.

sets.name: T
sets.doc: the hours of the day in chronological order, indexed by the integers 1 through 24; the first member is the opening hour and each later member follows the one before it

params.name: power_lim_wind
params.doc: the maximum wind power that can be generated in each hour, in power units

params.name: power_lim_pv
params.doc: the maximum solar (photovoltaic) power that can be generated in each hour, in power units

params.name: power_lim_fuel
params.doc: the maximum fuel-cell power that can be generated in each hour, in power units; equal to 80 for every hour

params.name: power_lim_charge
params.doc: the maximum power that can flow into the battery while charging in each hour, in power units; equal to 200 for every hour

params.name: power_lim_discharge
params.doc: the maximum power that can flow out of the battery while discharging in each hour, in power units; equal to 50 for every hour

params.name: power_lim_battery
params.doc: the maximum amount of energy the battery can hold in each hour, in power units; equal to 200 for every hour

params.name: initial_battery
params.doc: the amount of energy stored in the battery at the very start, before the first hour, in power units; a scalar equal to 100

params.name: load_demand
params.doc: the electricity load that must be served in each hour, in power units

params.name: cost_wind
params.doc: the unit cost of wind generation in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_pv
params.doc: the unit cost of solar generation in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_fuel

params.doc: the penalty cost for each unit of demand left unserved in each hour, in cost per power unit

params.name: cost_excess
params.doc: the unit cost charged for surplus generation that has to be dumped in each hour, in cost per power unit; equal to 0 for every hour

vars.name: battery
vars.doc: the energy stored in the battery at the end of each hour, in power units

vars.name: tc
vars.doc: the total operating cost over the whole day, in cost units

description: In every hour the energy stored in the battery at the end of the hour equals the energy it held coming into the hour, reduced by whatever was discharged and increased by whatever was charged that hour. The energy coming into the hour is the level carried over from the end of the previous hour, except in the very first hour where it is the starting battery level.

model_narrative: We operate a small power grid over a single day broken into 24 hourly periods. In each hour we decide how much power to generate from wind, solar, and a fuel cell, how much power to send into or draw out of a battery, and whether the battery is set to charge or to discharge that hour. We also account for any demand we fail to serve and any surplus generation that has to be dumped. Generation, charging, discharging, unserved demand, and surplus each carry their own per-unit cost that can vary by hour. The objective is to minimize the total operating cost over the day.

description: In every hour supply must match demand. The wind, solar, and fuel-cell generation together with the power discharged from the battery and any unserved demand must equal the load to be served plus the power used to charge the battery plus any surplus generation that is dumped.

model_narrative: We operate a small power grid over a single day broken into 24 hourly periods. In each hour we decide how much power to generate from wind, solar, and a fuel cell, how much power to send into or draw out of a battery, and whether the battery is set to charge or to discharge that hour. We also account for any demand we fail to serve and any surplus generation that has to be dumped. Generation, charging, discharging, unserved demand, and surplus each carry their own per-unit cost that can vary by hour. The objective is to minimize the total operating cost over the day.

vars.name: battery
vars.doc: the energy stored in the battery at the end of each hour, in power units

vars.name: tc
vars.doc: the total operating cost over the whole day, in cost units

description: The total operating cost is the sum over all hours of the cost of every activity that hour: wind, solar, and fuel-cell generation each valued at its own unit cost, charging and discharging each valued at its own unit cost, unserved demand valued at its penalty cost, and dumped surplus valued at its cost. Set the total cost variable equal to this sum.

model_narrative: We operate a small power grid over a single day broken into 24 hourly periods. In each hour we decide how much power to generate from wind, solar, and a fuel cell, how much power to send into or draw out of a battery, and whether the battery is set to charge or to discharge that hour. We also account for any demand we fail to serve and any surplus generation that has to be dumped. Generation, charging, discharging, unserved demand, and surplus each carry their own per-unit cost that can vary by hour. The objective is to minimize the total operating cost over the day.
